## 1. Destination Options — Where Can I Actually Go?

The honest answer is that SA engineers are better positioned to work abroad than almost any other profession leaving this country. The reason is a set of international agreements your degree has already unlocked — and most SA engineers do not know they exist.

---

### The Accord Advantage — Why Your SA Degree Travels

ECSA ([Engineering Council of South Africa](https://www.ecsa.co.za/accreditation-training/accreditation/)) is a full signatory to all three major international engineering accreditation accords administered by the [International Engineering Alliance](https://accreditation.org/accreditation-processes/accords/washington-accord): <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.ecsa.co.za/accreditation-training/accreditation/ | path=wiki/ECSA.md | claim_id=01-destinations-001 -->

- **Washington Accord** — mutual recognition of 4-year BEng/BE professional engineering degrees. SA signatory since 1999. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://accreditation.org/accreditation-processes/accords/washington-accord | path=wiki/Washington Accord.md | claim_id=01-destinations-002 -->
- **Sydney Accord** — mutual recognition of 3-year engineering technologist qualifications (BTech). SA signatory since 2001. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://accreditation.org/accreditation-processes/accords/washington-accord | path=wiki/ECSA.md | claim_id=01-destinations-003 -->
- **Dublin Accord** — mutual recognition of engineering technician qualifications. SA signatory since 2002. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://accreditation.org/accreditation-processes/accords/dublin-accord | path=wiki/ECSA.md | claim_id=01-destinations-004 -->

What this means in practice: the skills assessment bodies in Australia, New Zealand, the UK, Ireland, and Canada are all signatories to the same accords. When you apply for a skills assessment in those countries, your ECSA-accredited degree is recognised as substantially equivalent to a local degree — at the matching level. You do not start from zero.

**One critical check before you assume this applies to you:** your specific programme must be fully (not provisionally) accredited, and your graduation year must fall within the accredited intake period. Verify at the [IEA qualification checker](https://ieagreements.org) before paying any assessment fee. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://ieagreements.org | path=wiki/Washington Accord.md | claim_id=01-destinations-005 -->

---

### Route Status at a Glance *(May 2026)*

| Destination | Demand | Route Status | Difficulty for SA Engineers | Best For |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Australia** | High — 456 engineering occupations on shortage list | Open | Low-Medium | Employer sponsorship, long-term settlement |
| **New Zealand** | High — most engineering disciplines on shortage list | Open | Low-Medium | Fastest permanent residence |
| **United Kingdom** | High — chronic engineering shortages | Open | Medium | Large diaspora, strong agency support |
| **Ireland** | High — tech/pharma sector demand | Open, dual-step | Medium | Tax-efficient base, EU access |
| **Canada** | Moderate — STEM draws infrequent | Restricted | High (currently) | Long-term settlers willing to wait |
| **Germany** | High — 163+ shortage occupations | Open | High — language | German speakers only |

---

### Australia — Strongest Employer-Sponsored Route

Australia is the most reliable primary destination for SA engineers in 2025–2026.

**Demand:** Engineering occupations occupy a large portion of the [Core Skills Occupation List (CSOL)](https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/working-in-australia/skill-occupation-list), which has 456 eligible occupations. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/working-in-australia/skill-occupation-list | path=wiki/Engineers Australia.md | claim_id=01-destinations-006 --> The [Skills in Demand (SID) visa — Subclass 482, Core Skills stream](https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/skills-in-demand-482) launched on 7 December 2024, replacing the former TSS Subclass 482. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/skills-in-demand-482 | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-007 --> Minimum salary threshold: AUD $76,515 as of July 2025. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/working-in-australia/skill-occupation-list | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-008 -->

**The Accord advantage saves you money here:** [Engineers Australia (EA)](https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment) is the government-authorised skills assessment body for engineering occupations. SA engineers from ECSA-accredited BEng programmes apply via the Washington Accord pathway — AUD $539 (2025–26 financial year), versus AUD $1,001 for the Competency Demonstration Report (CDR) pathway. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=wiki/Engineers Australia.md | claim_id=01-destinations-009 --> That is approximately AUD $462 saved — and the CDR pathway also requires you to write lengthy engineering career episode reports, which can take months. The Accord pathway eliminates that entirely.

**Important:** not every engineering role is assessed by Engineers Australia. Some occupations — including Civil Engineering Draftsperson and certain technician roles — are assessed by VETASSESS or Trades Recognition Australia (TRA), not EA. Confirm the correct assessing authority for your exact ANZSCO occupation before paying any fee. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=docs/prompts/engineering/_BRIEF.md | claim_id=01-destinations-010 -->

**Fee increase notice (May 2026):** Engineers Australia has announced a 3–4% fee increase effective 1 July 2026. Fees listed here are confirmed for the 2025–26 financial year (source: Engineers Australia fee schedule, confirmed 25 July 2025). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=docs/prompts/engineering/_BRIEF.md | claim_id=01-destinations-011 -->

**Assessment processing time:** Standard processing takes 15+ weeks just to be assigned to an assessor, with total processing running 6–9 months in most cases. Fast-track add-on AUD $385 reduces post-assignment processing to approximately 2–4 weeks but does not eliminate the initial queue. EA outcome letters are valid for 3 years from issue (where no expiry date is printed). <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=wiki/Engineers Australia.md | claim_id=01-destinations-012 -->

**PR pathway:** SID 482 → Employer Nomination Scheme (ENS) Subclass 186 after 2 years of employment with the sponsoring employer. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/employer-nomination-scheme-186 | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-013 -->

**Honest assessment:** The highest-feasibility route of the five. Large SA engineering diaspora in Melbourne, Perth, and Brisbane. English-medium. The main constraint is finding a sponsoring employer — unlike New Zealand's Green List, you cannot apply for Australian PR without a job offer on the SID pathway. Budget 6–12 months for job search plus assessment before arrival.

---

### New Zealand — Best Direct-to-Residence Route

New Zealand offers something that no employer-sponsored route elsewhere matches: the ability to apply for permanent residence as your first visa, without serving a temporary-work period first.

**The Green List:** New Zealand's [Green List](https://www.immigration.govt.nz/new-zealand-visas/preparing-to-apply/working-in-new-zealand/green-list-occupations) covers virtually all engineering disciplines as Tier 1 (Straight to Residence). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.immigration.govt.nz/new-zealand-visas/preparing-to-apply/working-in-new-zealand/green-list-occupations | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-014 --> Confirmed Tier 1 disciplines include: civil, structural, mechanical, electrical, chemical, geotechnical, environmental, aeronautical, materials, electronics, telecommunications, naval architecture, and engineering technicians. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.immigration.govt.nz/new-zealand-visas/preparing-to-apply/working-in-new-zealand/green-list-occupations | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-015 --> A Tier 1 engineer with an accredited-employer job offer applies directly for a residence visa. No temporary visa first, no points competition.

**Wage threshold:** NZD $35/hour from 9 March 2026. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.immigration.govt.nz/new-zealand-visas/preparing-to-apply/working-in-new-zealand/green-list-occupations | path=docs/prompts/engineering/_BRIEF.md | claim_id=01-destinations-016 -->

**Age limit:** You must be 55 or under at the time of the residence application. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.immigration.govt.nz | path=docs/prompts/engineering/_BRIEF.md | claim_id=01-destinations-017 -->

**Credential recognition:** [Engineering New Zealand](https://www.engineeringnz.org) is the registration body. Immigration New Zealand explicitly recognises the Washington Accord pathway for SA BEng graduates from post-1999 ECSA-accredited programmes. The recommended first step is an [Engineering New Zealand Credential Check](https://www.engineeringnz.org) (NZD $550; 7–10 days) — this confirms your qualification meets NZ requirements before you commit to the full application. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineeringnz.org | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-018 -->

**Police clearance:** From December 2025, a pending-certificate receipt is not accepted — you need a valid police clearance certificate upfront at the time of application. Start the SAPS clearance + DIRCO apostille process early (allow 8–16 weeks combined). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.immigration.govt.nz | path=docs/prompts/engineering/_BRIEF.md | claim_id=01-destinations-019 -->

**Honest assessment:** Structurally the best destination for any SA engineer who prioritises speed to permanent residence and does not want to serve years on a temporary visa. Underrepresented in SA recruitment channels because the Australian pipeline is more established. NZ salaries are generally lower than Australian equivalents — research net pay by discipline and city before deciding.

---

### United Kingdom — Large Diaspora, Solid Route for Degree Engineers

The UK remains a strong primary option for SA engineers, with the best-developed recruitment infrastructure of any destination.

**Demand:** The UK has chronic engineering shortages across civil, structural, mechanical, and electrical disciplines. The [UK Register of Licensed Sponsors](https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/register-of-licensed-sponsors-workers) is publicly searchable — you can verify that a specific employer holds a Sponsor Licence before engaging with any recruiter. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/register-of-licensed-sponsors-workers | path=docs/prompts/engineering/_BRIEF.md | claim_id=01-destinations-020 -->

**July 2025 reforms — what changed for engineers:** From 22 July 2025, the Skilled Worker minimum skill threshold was raised from RQF 3 to RQF 6 (degree level). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/your-job | path=wiki/Visa Routes/UK Skilled Worker Visa.md | claim_id=01-destinations-021 --> Professional engineers with a degree already work at RQF 6 or above — this reform does not close the route for them. Sub-degree technician roles (RQF 3–5) are now restricted to roles listed on the Temporary Shortage List (TSL) or Immigration Salary List (ISL), which expire December 2026. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/your-job | path=wiki/Visa Routes/UK Skilled Worker Visa.md | claim_id=01-destinations-022 -->

**Current salary threshold:** £41,700 general minimum from 22 July 2025 (ONS-based). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/your-job | path=wiki/Visa Routes/UK Skilled Worker Visa.md | claim_id=01-destinations-023 --> Some engineering occupations have occupation-specific thresholds above the general floor — check [Appendix Skilled Occupations](https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/skilled-worker-visa-eligible-occupations) for your specific role. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/skilled-worker-visa-eligible-occupations | path=wiki/Visa Routes/UK Skilled Worker Visa.md | claim_id=01-destinations-024 -->

**English language:** South Africa is not on the UKVI English language exemption list. You have three options: (a) an IELTS Academic with an average of 4.0 or equivalent SELT; (b) a degree that was taught and assessed in English — a medium-of-instruction letter from your university is the practical route for most SA graduates; or (c) a B1 CEFR test. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/knowledge-of-english | path=wiki/Visa Routes/UK Skilled Worker Visa.md | claim_id=01-destinations-025 -->

**PR pathway:** Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) after 5 years of continuous UK residence on the Skilled Worker route. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/indefinite-leave-to-remain | path=wiki/Visa Routes/UK Skilled Worker Visa.md | claim_id=01-destinations-026 --> A 10-year "earned settlement" model was proposed in a Home Office consultation running November 2025 to February 2026. This has not been enacted into immigration rules as of May 2026 — treat it as a published policy proposal only, not confirmed law. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://knowledge.dlapiper.com/dlapiperknowledge/globalemploymentlatestdevelopments/2025/Changes-to-immigration-rules | path=wiki/Visa Routes/UK Skilled Worker Visa.md | claim_id=01-destinations-027 -->

**Additional recognition route (civil engineers):** The [SAICE](https://ice-sa.org.za/agreements-accords/) – [ICE (UK)](https://www.ice.org.uk) Mutual Exemption Agreement allows SAICE members registered with ECSA as Professional Engineers (with at least 1 year's standing) to obtain corresponding ICE membership and Chartered Engineer (CEng) registration in the UK. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://ice-sa.org.za/agreements-accords/ | path=docs/prompts/engineering/_BRIEF.md | claim_id=01-destinations-028 --> An equivalent mutual agreement for mechanical, electrical, and chemical engineers has not been confirmed from primary sources — check your professional institution directly.

**Honest assessment:** Still the most accessible first destination for most SA engineers. The largest SA engineering diaspora of any destination (London especially). Getting more competitive — the salary thresholds are significantly higher than they were three years ago and employer sponsorship remains a prerequisite.

---

### Ireland — Viable but Now Two Steps for SA Nationals

Ireland has strong demand from its tech, pharmaceutical, and medical device manufacturing sectors. It is a realistic route — but SA engineers must now complete two separate application processes, not one.

**The critical change (10 July 2024):** South Africa was added to Ireland's visa-required country list on 10 July 2024. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/permit-types/critical-skills-employment-permit/ | path=wiki/Visa Routes/Ireland Critical Skills Employment Permit.md | claim_id=01-destinations-029 --> An SA engineer now needs both a [Critical Skills Employment Permit (CSEP)](https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/permit-types/critical-skills-employment-permit/) and a separate Long Stay 'D' entry visa from the Irish Embassy or Consulate. The visa is applied for after the permit is approved. Do not plan this move assuming the permit alone is sufficient — it is no longer. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/permit-types/critical-skills-employment-permit/ | path=wiki/Visa Routes/Ireland Critical Skills Employment Permit.md | claim_id=01-destinations-030 -->

**Demand and permit scope:** Ireland issued 39,390 employment permits in 2024, up 27% year-on-year. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie | path=docs/prompts/engineering/_BRIEF.md | claim_id=01-destinations-031 --> South Africans received 1,631 of those permits, making SA the sixth-largest source country. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie | path=docs/prompts/engineering/_BRIEF.md | claim_id=01-destinations-032 --> Professional engineers and engineering technologists are explicitly listed on Ireland's [Critical Skills Occupations List (CSOL)](https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/employment-permit-eligibility/highly-skilled-eligible-occupations-list/). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/employment-permit-eligibility/highly-skilled-eligible-occupations-list/ | path=wiki/Visa Routes/Ireland Critical Skills Employment Permit.md | claim_id=01-destinations-033 -->

**No formal skills assessment required:** Unlike Australia and NZ, Ireland does not require an independent engineering skills assessment for the CSEP. Your degree and a qualifying job offer are the primary criteria. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/permit-types/critical-skills-employment-permit/ | path=wiki/Visa Routes/Ireland Critical Skills Employment Permit.md | claim_id=01-destinations-034 -->

**Salary threshold:** €40,904/year for CSOL-listed occupations (degree required). For occupations not on the CSOL, the threshold rises to €68,911. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/permit-types/critical-skills-employment-permit/ | path=wiki/Visa Routes/Ireland Critical Skills Employment Permit.md | claim_id=01-destinations-035 --> Recent graduates (within 12 months of graduation) have a reduced threshold of €36,848. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/permit-types/critical-skills-employment-permit/ | path=wiki/Visa Routes/Ireland Critical Skills Employment Permit.md | claim_id=01-destinations-036 -->

**Processing fee:** €1,000, with 90% refunded if the application is refused. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/permit-types/critical-skills-employment-permit/ | path=wiki/Visa Routes/Ireland Critical Skills Employment Permit.md | claim_id=01-destinations-037 -->

**PR pathway:** After the permit period (~2 years), Stamp 4 — the right to work in Ireland without a permit. Full long-term residence after 60 months. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/permit-types/critical-skills-employment-permit/ | path=wiki/Visa Routes/Ireland Critical Skills Employment Permit.md | claim_id=01-destinations-038 -->

**Combined processing time:** The CSEP processing timeline is published on enterprise.gov.ie and updated regularly (apply ≥12 weeks before your intended start date). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/permit-types/critical-skills-employment-permit/ | path=wiki/Visa Routes/Ireland Critical Skills Employment Permit.md | claim_id=01-destinations-039 --> The combined CSEP + Long Stay 'D' visa dual-track total is not officially published. Practitioners report 6–16 weeks total for the dual process — we could not confirm this from primary Irish government sources. Verify current timelines directly at [enterprise.gov.ie](https://enterprise.gov.ie) and [irishimmigration.ie](https://www.irishimmigration.ie) before making job-acceptance decisions. <!-- src: tier=unverified | url=https://www.enterprise.gov.ie | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-040 -->

**Honest assessment:** Ireland is a viable route with real demand — particularly for civil, structural, and mechanical engineers serving pharma and medtech projects. The dual-step SA visa requirement adds complexity and planning time. Family reunification rights are available from day one, which is an advantage over some other routes.

---

### Canada — Viable Long-Term, Restricted Right Now

Canada is not closed to SA engineers — but the specific draw route that was most accessible has become infrequent, and IRCC publishes no advance STEM draw schedule.

**STEM category draws:** Canada's Express Entry STEM category-based draws ran at lower CRS (Comprehensive Ranking System) thresholds of 430–480, making them accessible to many SA engineering candidates. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/rounds-invitations.html | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-041 --> **STEM category-based draws have been infrequent and IRCC publishes no advance schedule — do not count on one. Check the live rounds-of-invitations page for the current position.** <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/rounds-invitations.html | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-042 --> General Express Entry draws have run at CRS 470+, with a large standing pool of candidates. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/rounds-invitations.html | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-043 -->

**February 2025 STEM category change:** The STEM category was narrowed from 24 to 11 eligible occupations. Retained engineering occupations include Mechanical Engineers (NOC 21301) and Geological Engineers (NOC 21331), plus certain engineering technologist roles. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/rounds-invitations.html | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-044 --> Check [IRCC's current draw history page](https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/rounds-invitations.html) monthly — IRCC does not pre-announce draw schedules.

**The more reliable Canada pathway right now:** Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs). Ontario and Alberta have engineering-specific streams that add 600 CRS points to your Express Entry profile — effectively guaranteeing an Invitation to Apply when combined with a job offer or PNP nomination. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.ontario.ca/page/oinp-express-entry-human-capital-priorities-stream | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-045 --> Both programs have historically paused and restarted — verify current intake status directly at [Ontario OINP](https://www.ontario.ca/page/ontario-immigrant-nominee-program-oinp) and [Alberta AAIP](https://www.alberta.ca/aaip-overview.aspx). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.ontario.ca/page/ontario-immigrant-nominee-program-oinp | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-046 -->

**Credential recognition:** The [World Education Services (WES)](https://www.wes.org) Educational Credential Assessment (ECA) for Express Entry costs CAD $272 (from January 2026). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.wes.org/ca/wes-for-immigration-to-canada/ | path=docs/prompts/engineering/_BRIEF.md | claim_id=01-destinations-047 --> SA engineering degrees assess well through WES. Post-arrival P.Eng registration (province-level — PEO Ontario, APEGA Alberta, EGBC BC) is a post-arrival licensing step, not a pre-arrival immigration barrier.

**Canada is reducing intake targets post-2025** — draw frequency and volume may fall further before the next STEM category draw. Do not plan a Canada move around a STEM draw that IRCC has not scheduled — check the live rounds-of-invitations page before committing. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/news/2024/10/canada-to-welcome-fewer-permanent-residents-in-2025-to-stabilize-and-grow-the-economy.html | path=docs/prompts/engineering/_BRIEF.md | claim_id=01-destinations-048 -->

**Honest assessment:** Canada is the right choice for SA engineers who want a long-term North American base, are willing to wait for the right draw window or pursue a PNP, and have the budget and timeline flexibility that entails. It is not the right choice if you need to move in the next 6–12 months.

---

### Germany — Secondary Route, Language Required

Germany has 163+ shortage occupations as of 2026, and mechanical, civil, and electrical engineers qualify for the [EU Blue Card](https://www.make-it-in-germany.com/en/visa-residence/types/eu-blue-card) at a reduced threshold of €45,934/year (2026 rate). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.make-it-in-germany.com/en/visa-residence/types/eu-blue-card | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-049 -->

**The ZAB requirement:** A [ZAB Statement of Comparability](https://www.kmk.org/zab/central-office-for-foreign-education.html) is mandatory for SA engineers seeking regulated engineering roles in Germany. SA engineering degrees are not pre-listed in the Anabin database, meaning you cannot self-confirm comparability. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.kmk.org/zab/central-office-for-foreign-education.html | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-050 --> ZAB fee: €208; processing approximately 3 months. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.kmk.org/zab/central-office-for-foreign-education.html | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-051 -->

**The language barrier:** Most German engineering employment requires B2–C1 German. Non-regulated software/IT-adjacent engineering roles are an exception — they are not subject to state-level title recognition and can proceed with just a job offer above the salary threshold. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.make-it-in-germany.com/en/visa-residence/types/eu-blue-card | path=docs/prompts/engineering/_BRIEF.md | claim_id=01-destinations-052 -->

**The Opportunity Card (Chancenkarte):** Germany's Opportunity Card allows a 1-year job-search entry without a prior job offer for points-qualifying candidates. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.make-it-in-germany.com/en/visa-residence/types/opportunity-card | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-053 -->

**PR timeline:** Blue Card holders with B1 German can apply for permanent residence after 21 months. Without B1, the timeline is 27 months. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.make-it-in-germany.com/en/visa-residence/types/eu-blue-card | path=wa-engineering-01-destinations/FINAL_REPORT.md | claim_id=01-destinations-054 -->

**Honest assessment:** Germany is not a realistic primary destination unless you already have functional German. Engineers without German who explore Germany as an alternative to Australia or NZ typically underestimate the timeline — the language requirement and ZAB recognition process together add 12–18 months to the move. Do not prioritise Germany until you have at least B1 German.

---

### What Your Next Step Is

The ranking above reflects what the research actually supports, not what sounds appealing.

**If you want to move in the next 12 months:** Australia (employer sponsor search + EA Accord assessment) or New Zealand (Engineering NZ Credential Check → accredited-employer job search) are the two routes with the most reliable timelines and the clearest processes for SA engineers.

**If you are a civil engineer and the UK is your preference:** The ICE-SAICE Mutual Exemption Agreement gives you a direct professional recognition route that most other countries do not offer. Explore that in parallel with your job search.

**If Ireland interests you:** Start the job search before paying any fees. You need the employer and the CSEP in hand before the Long Stay 'D' entry visa process begins — reversing that order wastes money.

**If Canada is your long-term goal:** Enter your profile into the Express Entry pool and monitor IRCC's draw results page monthly. Do not pay immigration consultants to "guarantee" a STEM draw — IRCC does not pre-announce or guarantee draws.

**The information in this section is general guidance, not regulated immigration advice.** For decisions about your specific qualifications, work history, or visa application, consult an MARA-registered migration agent (Australia), an OISC-registered adviser (UK), or a Regulated Canadian Immigration Consultant (RCIC). Each country's professional adviser register is publicly searchable.

## 2. Document Checklist — What Papers Do I Need?

The document stack for engineering migration splits into two layers: SA-side preparation and destination-specific assessments. The SA-side layer is where most candidates lose months. Start it before you have a job offer — you will not be ready in time if you wait.

> **Critical path warning:** For Australia, the Engineers Australia skills assessment takes 15+ weeks just to be assigned to an assessor, with total processing running 6–9 months in most cases. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-001 --> The SAQA verification → DIRCO apostille chain takes a minimum of 6–8 weeks. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=DIRCO Apostille.md | claim_id=02-documents-002 --> The SAPS Police Clearance Certificate is valid for only 6 months from date of issue. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=Documents/SAPS Police Clearance Certificate.md | claim_id=02-documents-003 --> Plan these three timelines together from the start.

---

### SA-Side Documents (All Destinations)

These six documents are required regardless of which country you are targeting. Sequence them as listed — most have hard sequential dependencies.

| # | Document | Issued by | Cost | Processing | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Valid passport** | Department of Home Affairs | R1,000–R1,400 (indicative — confirm current fee at dha.gov.za before applying) <!-- src: tier=unverified | url=https://www.dha.gov.za | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-004 --> | 4–8 weeks (indicative — confirm current DHA processing times at dha.gov.za) <!-- src: tier=unverified | url=https://www.dha.gov.za | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-005 --> | Must be machine-readable and valid at least 6 months beyond your intended travel date. DHA queues are long — apply or renew before starting other steps. |
| 2 | **SAPS Police Clearance Certificate (PCC)** | SAPS Criminal Record Centre (CRC), Pretoria | ~R180 SAPS fee (2025) <!-- src: tier=unverified | url=https://apostil.co.za/police-clearance-saps/ | path=Documents/SAPS Police Clearance Certificate.md | claim_id=02-documents-006 -->; private agents R300–R1,500+ <!-- src: tier=unverified | url=https://apostil.co.za/police-clearance-saps/ | path=Documents/SAPS Police Clearance Certificate.md | claim_id=02-documents-007 --> | 5–30 working days (DIY); <!-- src: tier=unverified | url=https://apostil.co.za/police-clearance-saps/ | path=Documents/SAPS Police Clearance Certificate.md | claim_id=02-documents-008 --> 5–7 working days via agent <!-- src: tier=unverified | url=https://apostil.co.za/police-clearance-saps/ | path=Documents/SAPS Police Clearance Certificate.md | claim_id=02-documents-009 --> | Original signed and stamped copy only. Valid 6 months from issue — timing is critical (see trap section below). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=Documents/SAPS Police Clearance Certificate.md | claim_id=02-documents-010 --> Must be apostilled by DIRCO before submitting to any Hague Convention destination country (all five destinations qualify). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=DIRCO Apostille.md | claim_id=02-documents-011 --> **Exception: UK Skilled Worker visa does not typically require the SAPS PCC for engineering roles** — it is required for healthcare, education, and regulated social-care roles only. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/documents-you-must-provide | path=Documents/SAPS Police Clearance Certificate.md | claim_id=02-documents-012 --> Confirm with your UK employer and immigration adviser. |
| 3 | **DIRCO Apostille on PCC (and on SAQA letter)** | DIRCO, Pretoria | Free <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=DIRCO Apostille.md | claim_id=02-documents-013 --> | Same-day walk-in (online booking required; 60 slots per day, closed Wednesdays) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=DIRCO Apostille.md | claim_id=02-documents-014 -->; courier 3–4 weeks <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=DIRCO Apostille.md | claim_id=02-documents-015 --> | Cannot be obtained before the PCC exists — strictly sequential. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=DIRCO Apostille.md | claim_id=02-documents-016 --> DIRCO cannot apostille a raw degree certificate — it can only apostille the SAQA verification letter. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=DIRCO Apostille.md | claim_id=02-documents-017 --> |
| 4 | **SAQA Verification of National Qualifications** | South African Qualifications Authority | Fee not confirmed — saqa.org.za returned an SSL error during research and the two figures found ranged from ~R130 to ~R1,000. We could not resolve this; check saqa.org.za directly before applying. <!-- src: tier=unverified | url=https://www.saqa.org.za | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-018 --> | 25+ working days minimum <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.saqa.org.za | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-019 --> | DIRCO cannot apostille a degree certificate directly. SAQA must issue a verification letter first; DIRCO then apostilles that letter. This is the most commonly skipped step. Not the primary document for EA (Australia) — EA uses original transcripts from your university. Required for destinations that want an apostilled qualification. |
| 5 | **ECSA Confirmation of Registration / Good Standing letter** | Engineering Council of South Africa | Verify current fee at ecsa.co.za | 1–2 weeks <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.ecsa.co.za | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-020 --> | Required or strongly recommended at the EA skills assessment stage for Australia <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-021 --> and at destination professional registration. Confirms your current ECSA registration status. |
| 6 | **Degree certificate + academic transcripts** | SA university registrar | R500–R1,500 (indicative — varies by institution; confirm with your registrar before budgeting) <!-- src: tier=unverified | url=N/A | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-022 --> | 2–6 weeks (indicative — confirm with your registrar) <!-- src: tier=unverified | url=N/A | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-023 --> | Most assessors (EA, WES) and professional bodies require your institution to send transcripts directly — you cannot hand-deliver them. Contact your registrar early; backlogs are common at SA universities. |
| 7 | **English language test results** | IELTS / PTE Academic / OET | IELTS: ~R5,500–R5,700 (indicative — confirm current fee at britishcouncil.org.za before booking); <!-- src: tier=unverified | url=https://www.ielts.org | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-024 --> PTE Academic: similar; OET: ~R6,800 (confirm at occupationalenglishtest.org) <!-- src: tier=unverified | url=https://www.occupationalenglishtest.org | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-025 --> | Results ~13 days after sitting; valid 2 years <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.ielts.org | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-026 --> | Which test you need depends on your destination. **Do not sit standard IELTS Academic if applying for the UK Skilled Worker visa — IELTS for UKVI is required and it is a different test.** <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/knowledge-of-english | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-027 --> See per-country sections below. |

---

### The SAQA → DIRCO Chain: The Step Most People Miss

DIRCO is South Africa's competent authority under the Hague Apostille Convention. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=DIRCO Apostille.md | claim_id=02-documents-028 --> Two facts trip up most engineers:

**DIRCO cannot apostille your original degree certificate.** Your degree was issued by a private institution, not by a competent SA public authority. DIRCO only apostilles documents that were themselves issued or verified by a recognised SA authority. Your degree must first go through SAQA, which issues a verification letter; DIRCO then apostilles that SAQA letter. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=DIRCO Apostille.md | claim_id=02-documents-029 --> Skip this step and you cannot produce an apostilled qualification certificate at a later stage.

**SAQA takes 25+ working days minimum.** <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.saqa.org.za | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-030 --> Add 3–4 weeks for the courier apostille route at DIRCO, and the total SA qualification chain is 6–10 weeks in practice. This is the SA-side bottleneck for NZ, Ireland, and supplementary documentation for other destinations.

Note: For Australia (EA skills assessment), EA primarily wants original degree certificates and transcripts sent directly from your university — the apostilled SAQA letter is not EA's primary requirement. For Canada (WES ECA), WES requires transcripts sent directly from your university. For the UK (Ecctis route), you apply online and no SAQA apostille is needed. The SAQA → DIRCO chain matters most when a destination explicitly requires an apostilled credential certificate.

---

### The PCC Timing Trap

The SAPS PCC is valid for only 6 months from the date of issue. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=Documents/SAPS Police Clearance Certificate.md | claim_id=02-documents-031 --> If you obtain it now and your visa application runs long, it expires and you restart. Do not apply for the PCC until your visa application is within six months of likely lodgement.

For Australia: start the EA skills assessment first (15+ weeks to assessor assignment alone), <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-032 --> then schedule your PCC to arrive when the EA outcome is approaching. The PCC and DIRCO apostille together take 4–8 weeks total. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=DIRCO Apostille.md | claim_id=02-documents-033 -->

A secondary trap: SAPS officers who sign PCCs must have their signatures registered with DIRCO. If the signing officer's signature is not on DIRCO's register, the apostille is refused and the process restarts. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://apostil.co.za/police-clearance-saps/ | path=Documents/SAPS Police Clearance Certificate.md | claim_id=02-documents-034 --> Using a private expedited agent who works through known CRC officers reduces this risk.

---

### Australia: Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment

The EA Migration Skills Assessment (MSA) is the mandatory prerequisite for all points-tested and employer-sponsored skilled migration pathways to Australia. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-035 --> No SkillSelect Expression of Interest can be submitted without a positive EA outcome letter.

**Step 1: Confirm your ANZSCO occupation and assessing authority.** Not all engineering occupations are assessed by Engineers Australia. Civil Engineering Draftsperson and certain technician roles are assessed by VETASSESS or Trades Recognition Australia (TRA), not EA. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-036 --> Paying for an EA assessment under the wrong ANZSCO code wastes the fee and delays your application.

**Step 2: Determine your pathway — Accord or CDR.**

SA is a full Washington Accord signatory since 1999. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.internationalengineeringalliance.org/ | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-037 --> This means graduates of qualifying ECSA-accredited programmes may use the Accord pathway (no Competency Demonstration Report required) instead of the CDR pathway.

**Accord eligibility is not automatic for every ECSA-accredited degree.** All three conditions must be met simultaneously:

- SA was accepted as a full signatory before your graduation year — the 1999 date covers most SA engineering graduates; <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.internationalengineeringalliance.org/ | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-038 -->
- Your specific programme (not just your university) is listed as **fully accredited** (not provisionally) on the IEA qualification checker; <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.internationalengineeringalliance.org/for-engineers/qualification-checker | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-039 -->
- Your graduation year falls within the listed accreditation period. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.internationalengineeringalliance.org/for-engineers/qualification-checker | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-040 -->

Check your programme at the [IEA Qualification Checker](https://www.internationalengineeringalliance.org/for-engineers/qualification-checker) before submitting. This 5-minute check determines whether you pay AUD $539 or AUD $1,001 and whether you spend weeks writing Career Episodes.

**EA fees (Engineers Australia fee schedule, updated July 2025 — a 3–4% increase is confirmed for 1 July 2026):** <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-041 -->

| Pathway | AUD fee (July 2025) | Approx. ZAR equivalent* |
|---|---|---|
| Washington/Sydney/Dublin Accord pathway | AUD $539 | ~R6,490 |
| CDR pathway (full Competency Demonstration Report) | AUD $1,001 | ~R12,050 |
| Fast Track add-on (EA portal) | AUD $385 | ~R4,640 |
| Skilled employment assessment (separate) | AUD $517 | ~R6,230 |

*ZAR approximations based on AUD/ZAR ~12.04 as of May 2026 (consistent with the rate block in Section 3) — check current rate before budgeting. <!-- src: tier=unverified | url=https://www.xe.com | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-042 --> Verify current fees at [engineersaustralia.org.au](https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment) before submitting, given the July 2026 increase. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-043 -->

**Processing:** Standard processing takes 15 weeks to be assigned to an assessor, with actual assessment time additional — budget 6–9 months for the full EA process. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-044 --> The EA outcome letter is treated as valid for 3 years from date of issue by the Australian Department of Home Affairs. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/ | path=Department of Home Affairs Australia.md | claim_id=02-documents-045 -->

**Documents for the Accord pathway:**
- Certified degree certificate and academic transcripts (from your institution)
- IELTS Academic or PTE Academic results (minimum 6.0 each component) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-046 -->
- Employment references confirming engineering work experience
- Passport (identity)
- ECSA Confirmation of Registration (supporting evidence; requested by EA in some cases) <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-047 -->

**Additional documents for CDR pathway (if Accord pathway not available):**
- Competency Demonstration Report: three Career Episodes (1,000–2,500 words each) plus a Summary Statement <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-048 -->
- Continuing Professional Development (CPD) list
- EA runs a plagiarism check on CDRs — unique, first-person narrative required; do not use template-writing services <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment | path=Assessments/Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-049 -->

**Additional documents for the Australian visa application (Subclass 189/190/482):**
- Positive EA skills assessment outcome letter
- SAPS PCC (apostilled by DIRCO) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/ | path=Department of Home Affairs Australia.md | claim_id=02-documents-050 -->
- Police clearances from all countries where you have resided for 12 months or more <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-051 -->
- Health examination through an approved immigration medical panel (IOM MHAC Pretoria is the most accessible SA centre) <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-052 -->
- Form 80 (Personal Particulars for Character Assessment) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-053 -->

---

### UK: Skilled Worker Visa Documents

For the UK Skilled Worker visa, the document load is lighter than the EA process — but two SA-specific traps have caught out many candidates.

**Trap 1: Standard IELTS Academic is not accepted.** If you are applying for a Skilled Worker visa, you must sit IELTS for UKVI (or another UKVI-approved English test) at a UK Visas and Immigration approved test centre. Standard IELTS Academic, taken at a regular British Council centre, is not accepted by UKVI. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/knowledge-of-english | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-054 --> SA has UKVI-approved test centres. Confirm the correct test code when booking.

**Trap 2: Your SA degree needs Ecctis recognition, not an apostille.** For the UK Skilled Worker visa, your SA engineering qualification must be assessed via [Ecctis](https://ecctis.com/) (the UK government's recognised equivalency service), which issues a UK ENIC Qualification Equivalency Letter with a reference number. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/documents-you-must-provide | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-055 --> This is an online application — no physical SAQA verification or DIRCO apostille is needed for the UK visa route. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/documents-you-must-provide | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-056 -->

**UK Skilled Worker visa documents checklist:**
- Valid passport
- Certificate of Sponsorship (CoS) reference number from your UK licensed employer <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/documents-you-must-provide | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-057 -->
- IELTS for UKVI results at B2 level <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/knowledge-of-english | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-058 -->
- Ecctis UK ENIC Qualification Equivalency Letter (reference number)
- Employment and salary evidence (payslips, employment contracts where required) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/documents-you-must-provide | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-059 -->
- SAPS PCC: **not a standard requirement for engineering Skilled Worker visa applications** (required for healthcare, education, and regulated social-care roles) <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/documents-you-must-provide | path=Documents/SAPS Police Clearance Certificate.md | claim_id=02-documents-060 --> — confirm with your employer and immigration adviser
- South Africa is not on the UK TB test list — no TB clearance certificate is required <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/tb-test-visa | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-061 -->

Note: CEng/IEng registration with the Engineering Council UK is optional and is a post-arrival professional development step, not a visa requirement. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engc.org.uk/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-062 --> SAICE members registered with ECSA as Professional Engineers with at least 1 year standing may access a Mutual Exemption Agreement pathway to ICE membership in the UK. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.ice.org.uk/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-063 -->

---

### New Zealand: Green List / Accredited Employer Work Visa Documents

Most professional engineering disciplines sit on the NZ Green List as Tier 1 (Straight to Residence), <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.immigration.govt.nz/new-zealand-visas/preparing-a-visa-application/working-in-nz/green-list | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-064 --> meaning a job offer from an accredited NZ employer leads directly to residence without a temporary visa period. Verify the current Tier 1 status of your specific discipline at immigration.govt.nz — the list is updated periodically.

**Critical document trap: police certificate must be the actual certificate, not a receipt.** From December 2025, Immigration NZ no longer accepts a pending-certificate receipt in place of the completed SAPS PCC. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.immigration.govt.nz | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-065 --> Apply for the PCC and DIRCO apostille early enough that the final certificate arrives before you lodge your application — while still watching the 6-month validity window.

**Age eligibility:** applicants must be 55 or younger at the time of the residence application. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.immigration.govt.nz | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-066 -->

**Engineering New Zealand (ENZ) Credential Check:** ENZ verifies your SA degree against the Washington Accord and issues a letter used in accredited employer applications and Skilled Migrant Category Resident Visa submissions. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.engineeringnz.org | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-067 --> Processing is 7–10 working days. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.engineeringnz.org | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-068 --> The fee for non-ENZ members is not publicly listed — confirm at [engineeringnz.org](https://www.engineeringnz.org/join-us/fees/) before submitting.

**NZQA International Qualification Assessment (IQA):** May be required unless your SA BEng/BSc(Eng) degree appears on the NZ List of Qualifications Exempt from Assessment (LQEA). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.nzqa.govt.nz | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-069 --> Whether ECSA-accredited Washington Accord programmes currently appear on the LQEA exemption list was not confirmed from primary sources at time of writing (May 2026) — check [nzqa.govt.nz](https://www.nzqa.govt.nz/qualifications-standards/international-qualifications/nzqa-qualifications-framework/lqea/) directly before paying for the IQA.

**NZ documents checklist:**
- Valid passport
- Job offer from an NZ-accredited employer at a minimum wage of NZD $35/hour (from 9 March 2026) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.immigration.govt.nz | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-070 -->
- ENZ Credential Check letter (where required by Immigration NZ)
- IELTS 6.5 minimum per band (General or Academic) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.immigration.govt.nz | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-071 -->
- SAPS PCC (apostilled by DIRCO) — must be the completed certificate, not a pending receipt <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.immigration.govt.nz | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-072 -->
- Health examination through an approved Immigration NZ panel doctor <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.immigration.govt.nz | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-073 -->

---

### Ireland: Critical Skills Employment Permit Documents

Ireland's Critical Skills Employment Permit (CSEP) does not require pre-arrival professional registration — Engineers Ireland membership is optional and is a post-arrival step, not a condition of the permit. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-074 --> The pre-arrival document load is lighter than Australia or NZ.

**Critical trap: SA nationals have needed both the CSEP and a Long Stay 'D' entry visa since 10 July 2024.** <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.irishimmigration.ie/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-075 --> The employment permit alone is not sufficient. The Long Stay 'D' entry visa is a separate application to the Irish Naturalisation and Immigration Service (INIS). Confirm the current application requirements on [irishimmigration.ie](https://www.irishimmigration.ie/) before making any travel arrangements.

**Ireland documents checklist:**
- Valid passport
- Formal job offer from a bona-fide Irish employer, salary at minimum €40,904 for engineering roles on the Critical Skills Occupations List, with a 2-year minimum contract <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-076 -->
- Evidence of engineering qualification and relevant employment experience (submitted with the CSEP application form) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-077 -->
- CSEP application submitted at least 12 weeks before your employment start date <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-078 -->
- Long Stay 'D' entry visa application (INIS — separate from the CSEP) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.irishimmigration.ie/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-079 -->
- SAPS PCC (for the visa application) <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.irishimmigration.ie/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-080 -->
- No mandatory English language test is required by the Department of Enterprise for the CSEP itself <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-081 --> — individual employers set their own requirements

The CSEP fee is €1,000. By law, this cost must be borne by the employer — it is illegal for it to be passed on to you. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-082 --> Do not travel to Ireland or resign from your SA position before you have both the CSEP and the Long Stay 'D' visa in hand.

---

### Canada: Express Entry / WES ECA Documents

For Canada Express Entry (Federal Skilled Workers Program), the credential assessment is a WES Educational Credential Assessment (ECA) — not a professional skills assessment like EA. The WES ECA confirms your SA engineering degree is equivalent to a Canadian credential. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/documents/education-assessment.html | path=Assessments/WES Educational Credential Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-083 -->

P.Eng provincial licensure (through PEO Ontario, APEGA, EGBC, or similar) is entirely separate and is a post-arrival process — it is not a pre-migration requirement and does not affect your Express Entry eligibility. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/ | path=Assessments/WES Educational Credential Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-084 -->

**WES ECA critical trap: your SA university must send transcripts directly.** WES requires official transcripts sent from your registrar to WES — you cannot submit them yourself. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.wes.org/eca/ | path=Assessments/WES Educational Credential Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-085 --> Some SA universities charge advance fees, have long turnaround times, or trigger a verification hold with WES that delays the assessment by months. Contact your registrar before applying to WES.

**WES ECA details:**
- Fee: CAD $272 plus document delivery costs (from January 2026) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.wes.org/evaluations-and-fees/fees/ | path=Assessments/WES Educational Credential Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-086 --> — verify current fee schedule at [wes.org/eca-2](https://www.wes.org/eca-2/)
- Processing: WES publishes current turnaround times at [wes.org/current-processing](https://www.wes.org/current-processing/) — allow 4–12 weeks plus courier time <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.wes.org/current-processing/ | path=Assessments/WES Educational Credential Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-087 -->
- Validity: 5 years from date of issue <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.wes.org/eca/ | path=Assessments/WES Educational Credential Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-088 -->

**English test for Canada: IELTS General Training only.** IELTS General Training (not IELTS Academic) is the accepted format for IRCC Express Entry. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-089 --> PTE Academic is **not accepted** for IRCC immigration purposes. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-090 --> Minimum CLB 7 across all four abilities is required for Federal Skilled Worker Program eligibility. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-091 -->

**Canada Express Entry documents checklist:**
- Valid passport
- WES ECA letter (valid within 5 years of application) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/ | path=Assessments/WES Educational Credential Assessment.md | claim_id=02-documents-092 -->
- IELTS General Training results (CLB 7 minimum) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-093 -->
- SAPS PCC (apostilled by DIRCO) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-094 -->
- Proof of settlement funds — IRCC publishes current minimums at canada.ca <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/documents/proof-funds.html | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-095 -->
- Employment references confirming work experience (for CRS points) <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/immigrate-canada/express-entry/ | path=N/A | claim_id=02-documents-096 -->

---

### Key Traps Summary

| Trap | Destinations affected | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| Obtaining the SAPS PCC too early | All | 6-month validity expires before visa lodgement; must reapply and restart |
| Submitting transcripts yourself to WES or EA instead of via the registrar | Australia (EA), Canada (WES) | Application rejected or held; weeks of delay |
| Sitting standard IELTS Academic for UK Skilled Worker visa | UK | Test not accepted by UKVI; must resit at a UKVI-approved centre with the correct test format |
| Assuming Accord pathway eligibility without checking IEA qualifier | Australia | EA assesses under CDR pathway; higher fee and months of CDR writing |
| Not checking ANZSCO occupation against the correct assessing authority | Australia | Paying EA for an assessment that should go to VETASSESS or Trades Recognition Australia |
| Skipping SAQA → DIRCO chain when a destination requests an apostilled degree certificate | NZ, Ireland (where required) | Cannot produce apostilled qualification without SAQA step; weeks of delay |
| Travelling to Ireland or resigning before receiving both the CSEP and Long Stay 'D' visa | Ireland | Serious financial and legal exposure |
| Using PTE Academic for Canada Express Entry | Canada | PTE Academic not accepted by IRCC; must resit IELTS General Training |
| Assuming P.Eng is needed before applying to Canada | Canada | P.Eng is a post-arrival provincial process — not a pre-migration prerequisite |
| Submitting a pending-certificate receipt instead of the completed SAPS PCC | New Zealand | Application rejected (from December 2025) |

---

> **This section is general information only, not immigration advice.** Every applicant's circumstances differ. Consult an OISC-registered adviser (UK), MARA-registered agent (Australia), or equivalent licensed adviser in your destination country for advice specific to your case.

## 3. Realistic Costs — How Much Will This Actually Cost Me?

All ZAR equivalents below use May 2026 indicative rates: £1 = R22.73 / AUD$1 = R12.04 / €1 = R19.51 / NZD$1 = R10.94 / CAD$1 ≈ R13.10. Exchange rates move — treat every ZAR figure as a planning estimate, not a guarantee. All foreign-currency fees are cited from official government or regulator sources.

---

### SA-Side Costs (Every Destination)

These costs apply regardless of which country you are applying to. Do not wait for a job offer to start this process — budget 8–12 weeks for the full SA documentation cycle. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.saps.gov.za/services/applying_clearence_certificate.php | path=SA Documentation — Full Package | claim_id=03-costs-001 -->

| Item | ZAR | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| SAPS Police Clearance Certificate (government fee) | R180 | <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.saps.gov.za/services/applying_clearence_certificate.php | path=SA Documentation — Full Package | claim_id=03-costs-002 --> |
| SAPS PCC — total realistic cost (apostille + couriers included) | R4,000–R5,500 | <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.saps.gov.za/services/applying_clearence_certificate.php | path=SA Documentation — Full Package | claim_id=03-costs-003 --> |
| DIRCO apostille per additional document (degree certificate, etc.) | ~R2,500 each | Fee not officially published in accessible online schedule — confirm at dirco.gov.za <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.burgerhuyserattorneys.co.za/how-much-does-it-cost-to-apostille-a-document-in-south-africa/ | path=Cost Items/DIRCO Apostille Fee | claim_id=03-costs-004 --> |
| 2–3 apostilles total (typical budget) | R5,000–R7,500 | <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=Cost Items/DIRCO Apostille Fee | claim_id=03-costs-005 --> |
| IELTS Academic (one attempt, British Council SA) | R5,500–R6,200 | Confirm current rate at britishcouncil.org/south-africa <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.britishcouncil.org.za/exam/ielts | path=Cross-Destination Cost Comparison | claim_id=03-costs-006 --> |
| PTE Academic (cheaper — valid for UK/Australia; for Canada Express Entry use PTE Core or IELTS General Training instead) | R4,100 | <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://www.pearsonpte.com/pte-academic | path=Cross-Destination Cost Comparison | claim_id=03-costs-007 --> |
| SAQA qualification verification | R168 per tertiary qualification record (standard 6–25 working days, no fieldwork); R526 if fieldwork is required. A stand-alone SAQA verification letter is R713. Tariffs rise again on 1 April 2027. | Required by some destination assessors — confirm the current tariff at saqa.org.za before applying <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://saqa.org.za/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/2026-to-2028-National-Verification-Tariffs.pdf | path=SA Documentation — Full Package | claim_id=03-costs-008 --> |
| ECSA annual registration fee (only if you register as Pr Eng — most engineers do not need to) | R5,713/year for Professional categories incl. Pr Eng (R4,982 with a voluntary-association partial exemption); R2,310/year for Candidate categories. Reviewed every April. | A separate optional cost, not part of the SA-side total below — see Section 6 on whether you need Pr Eng at all <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://ecsa.co.za/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Annual-and-application-fee-from-01-May-2025-to-31-March-2026.pdf | path=SA Documentation — Full Package | claim_id=03-costs-009 --> |
| **SA-side total** | **R13,000–R21,000** | <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.saps.gov.za | path=SA Documentation — Full Package | claim_id=03-costs-010 --> |

DIRCO no longer accepts walk-in submissions as of 2025 — all documents must be submitted via courier or a DIRCO-registered agency. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=Cost Items/DIRCO Apostille Fee | claim_id=03-costs-011 --> Standard DIRCO processing time is 3–4 weeks. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://dirco.gov.za/consular-notarial-services/ | path=Cost Items/DIRCO Apostille Fee | claim_id=03-costs-012 --> Your SAPS PCC is valid for six months from the date of issue — time your application so the certificate does not expire before you submit your visa. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.saps.gov.za/services/applying_clearence_certificate.php | path=SA Documentation — Full Package | claim_id=03-costs-013 -->

---

### Before You Pay Engineers Australia: Check IEA Eligibility First

This is the highest-value 10 minutes in this guide if Australia is on your shortlist.

Engineers Australia (EA) runs two main assessment pathways. The **Accord pathway** — for degrees recognised under the Washington, Sydney, or Dublin Accord — costs AUD $539 including GST (~R6,490). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment/assessment-fees-and-additional-services | path=Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment | claim_id=03-costs-014 --> The **CDR pathway** — a Competency Demonstration Report, required when a degree is not covered by an Accord — costs AUD $1,001 including GST (~R12,050). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment/assessment-fees-and-additional-services | path=Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment | claim_id=03-costs-015 --> The gap between the two assessment fees alone is AUD $462 (~R5,560). <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment/assessment-fees-and-additional-services | path=Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment | claim_id=03-costs-016 --> Engineers who need a professional CDR writer should budget an additional AUD $700–3,000 (~R8,430–R36,120) for that service on top of the CDR pathway fee — a complete report typically runs AUD $700–1,300, with budget providers advertising from ~AUD $365 and premium fast-turnaround packages reaching AUD $2,500–3,000. These are unregulated commercial services; get a written quote and check reviews before paying. <!-- src: tier=secondary | url=https://cdrwritersaustralia.com/cdr-writing-prices/ | path=Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment | claim_id=03-costs-017 -->

South Africa has been a Washington Accord signatory since 1999. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://ieagreements.org | path=IEA Qualification Checker | claim_id=03-costs-018 --> Most ECSA-accredited 4-year BEng degrees qualify for the cheaper Accord pathway — but ECSA accreditation alone does not confirm it. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://ieagreements.org | path=IEA Qualification Checker | claim_id=03-costs-019 --> Eligibility depends on the specific programme name, awarding institution, and graduation year. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://ieagreements.org | path=IEA Qualification Checker | claim_id=03-costs-020 --> Degrees awarded before 1999 may not qualify under the Washington Accord. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://ieagreements.org | path=IEA Qualification Checker | claim_id=03-costs-021 -->

Check the IEA qualifier at [ieagreements.org](https://ieagreements.org) before submitting anything to Engineers Australia. It takes 10 minutes and potentially saves R5,560–R41,680 depending on whether a CDR writer is also needed.

**Fee increase from 1 July 2026:** Engineers Australia has announced a 3–4% fee increase effective 1 July 2026. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment/assessment-fees-and-additional-services | path=Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment | claim_id=03-costs-022 --> Post-July 2026, the Accord pathway rises to approximately AUD $556 including GST, and the CDR pathway rises to approximately AUD $1,034 including GST. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment/assessment-fees-and-additional-services | path=Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment | claim_id=03-costs-023 -->

**Not all engineering roles are assessed by EA.** Some occupations — for example, Civil Engineering Draftsperson and certain technician roles — are assessed by VETASSESS or Trades Recognition Australia, not Engineers Australia. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/working-in-australia/skill-occupation-list | path=Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment | claim_id=03-costs-024 --> Confirm the correct assessing authority for your exact ANZSCO occupation code at the [Department of Home Affairs website](https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au) before paying any assessment fee. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au | path=Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment | claim_id=03-costs-025 -->

---

### Australia — Cost Scenarios

The Skills in Demand (SID) visa, subclass 482, replaced the Temporary Skill Shortage visa on 7 December 2024. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/skills-in-demand-482 | path=Cross-Destination Cost Comparison | claim_id=03-costs-026 -->

| Cost Item | Foreign currency | ZAR (May 2026) | Paid by |
|---|---|---|---|
| EA skills assessment — Accord pathway | AUD $539 incl. GST | ~R6,490 | Engineer |
| EA skills assessment — CDR pathway | AUD $1,001 incl. GST | ~R12,050 | Engineer |
| CDR writing service (if required) | AUD $700–3,000 | ~R8,430–R36,120 | Engineer |
| SID 482 visa — principal applicant (from 1 July 2025) | AUD $3,210 | ~R38,650 | Often employer |
| Skilling Australians Fund (SAF) levy | AUD $1,200–1,800/year | — | **Employer only — cannot be charged to engineer** |
| Employer nomination fee | AUD $330 | — | **Employer only — cannot be charged to engineer** |
| Visa medical examination | ~AUD $400 | ~R4,820 | Engineer |
| First month, Melbourne shared room (indicative) | ~AUD $2,000–2,700/month | ~R24,080–R32,510 | Engineer |
| First month, Sydney shared room (indicative) | ~AUD $2,500–3,200/month | ~R30,100–R38,530 | Engineer |
| Return flights SA to Australia | — | R16,000–R26,000 | Sometimes employer |

<!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/skills-in-demand-482 | path=Cross-Destination Cost Comparison | claim_id=03-costs-027 -->
<!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.engineersaustralia.org.au/migrants/migration-skills-assessment/assessment-fees-and-additional-services | path=Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment | claim_id=03-costs-028 -->

EA assessment validity: if no expiry date is printed on the outcome letter, the Department of Home Affairs treats a skills assessment as valid for three years from issue. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au | path=Engineers Australia Migration Skills Assessment | claim_id=03-costs-029 -->

| Scenario | Total out-of-pocket | Assumptions |
|---|---|---|
| **Low** | ~R70,000 | Accord pathway; employer covers SID 482 visa; Melbourne; one PTE attempt |
| **Mid** | ~R115,000 | Accord pathway; engineer pays SID 482 visa; Melbourne; IELTS |
| **High** | ~R165,000+ | CDR pathway + mid-range CDR writer; engineer pays visa; Sydney; IELTS |

---

### United Kingdom — Cost Scenarios

The UK Skilled Worker Visa skill threshold was raised to RQF 6 from July 2025. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa | path=Cross-Destination Cost Comparison | claim_id=03-costs-030 --> Four-year degree engineers are unaffected; sub-degree technician roles were closed to new applicants from that date. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa | path=Cross-Destination Cost Comparison | claim_id=03-costs-031 --> The general salary threshold is £41,700 or the published going rate for the occupation, whichever is higher. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/your-job | path=Cross-Destination Cost Comparison | claim_id=03-costs-032 --> A 10-year "earned settlement" model — raising the standard settlement (ILR) qualifying period from 5 to 10 years — was put to a Home Office public consultation that ran from 20 November 2025 to 12 February 2026. As of May 2026 the government had not published its response and nothing had been enacted into the Immigration Rules. Treat it as a firm policy direction, not confirmed law, and check the gov.uk "Earned settlement" consultation page for the published outcome before making any plans that depend on settlement timing. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/government/consultations/earned-settlement | path=Cross-Destination Cost Comparison | claim_id=03-costs-033 -->

| Cost Item | Foreign currency | ZAR (May 2026) | Paid by |
|---|---|---|---|
| Skilled Worker Visa — 3 years, applied outside UK | £819 | ~R18,620 | Often employer |
| Immigration Health Surcharge (IHS) — 3 years per person | £3,105 (£1,035/year) | ~R70,600 | Often employer in engineering |
| Skills assessment fee | None | — | — |
| First month, London shared room (indicative) | ~£1,000–1,200/month | ~R22,730–R27,280 | Engineer |
| First month, outside London shared room (indicative) | ~£700–900/month | ~R15,910–R20,460 | Engineer |
| Return flights SA to UK | — | R12,000–R18,000 | Sometimes employer |

<!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/how-much-it-costs | path=Cross-Destination Cost Comparison | claim_id=03-costs-034 -->

**IHS — current rate, and a common confusion to avoid:** The Immigration Health Surcharge is £1,035 per person per year for most adult applicants (£776/year for under-18 dependants), unchanged since February 2024 and still current as of May 2026. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/healthcare-immigration-application/how-much-pay | path=Cross-Destination Cost Comparison | claim_id=03-costs-035 --> The widely reported 32% increase announced in the May 2025 UK Immigration White Paper applies to the **Immigration Skills Charge** — a levy employers pay to sponsor a worker, which rose to £1,320 per year for large sponsors from 16 December 2025 — not to the health surcharge you pay yourself. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/restoring-control-over-the-immigration-system-white-paper/restoring-control-over-the-immigration-system-accessible | path=Cross-Destination Cost Comparison | claim_id=03-costs-036 --> Always confirm the current IHS rate at [gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/how-much-it-costs](https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/how-much-it-costs) before submitting your application, as fees are reviewed periodically.

| Scenario | Total out-of-pocket | Assumptions |
|---|---|---|
| **Low** | ~R55,000 | Employer covers visa + IHS; outside London; one IELTS attempt |
| **Mid** | ~R120,000 | Engineer pays IHS (£3,105); employer covers visa; outside London |
| **High** | ~R150,000+ | Engineer pays visa + IHS at current rate; London accommodation |

---

### Ireland — Cost Scenarios

SA citizens have been required to hold a visa to enter Ireland since 10 July 2024. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://www.irishimmigration.ie/coming-to-work-in-ireland/what-are-my-options-for-working-in-ireland/ | path=Cross-Destination Cost Comparison | claim_id=03-costs-037 --> An engineer needs both a **Critical Skills Employment Permit (CSEP)** and a **Long Stay 'D' entry visa** — the permit alone does not authorise entry to Ireland. <!-- src: tier=primary | url=https://enterprise.gov.ie/en/what-we-do/workplace-and-skills/employment-permits/ | path=Cross-Destination Cost Comparison | claim_id=03-costs-038 -->

| Cost Item | Foreign currency | ZAR (May 2026) | Paid by |
|---|---|---|---|
| Critical Skills Employment Permit | €1,000 (90% refunded if refused) | ~R19,510 | **Employer — it is illegal for the employer to pass this cost to the employee** |
